rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Lockheed Martin Tactical Systems, Inc.¿s, Meggitt Aircraft Braking Systems Corporation¿s and The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is denied without prejudice as untimely. Defendant¿s proof of service demonstrates that defendant failed to provide proper notice to Mr. Frazier as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Duro Dyne Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate The Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc.¿s joinder in Duro Dyne Corporation¿s motion to bifurcate the trial is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 1005(b), C.C.P. 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. Plaintiff¿s objection is sustained. The joinder is denied without prejudice to defendant filing its own motion and providing proper notice. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff, And Granting Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5.
